other_observers: none; Toni Brevalier  also seen by David Chartier, Brad, Andrew, and Sophie Steger, Mark Peteson, Lisa Edwards, and Scott Schaum.
specific_location: Retirement community on Lower Gold Camp Road just east of Bear Creek County Park.
description: Red or Taiga Fox Sparrow  Rare in El Paso County  A large sparrow with an all red tail and reddish stripes on the breast with a large reddish "stick pin". The wigs and wing coverts were reddish except darker gray wing tips. Flanks with broad rusty streaks. Head was red and gray, the auricualar red, broad gray supercillium and rustly crown. The nape and upper back had a medium gray back with broad rusty verticle streaks. The bill was stout and yellow based on the lower and upper mandibles with dusky towards the tip and along the culmen. The legs were thick and pink.Behaviors: Fed under a feeder in a surburban yard. Double-jumped, scratching in the stones to uncover provided food of millet, and sunflower seeds.Call: none heardPlumage: adult?
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
